positive_cases.expected_issue: FILTER inside CALCULATE for simple equality

positive_cases.name: FILTER inside CALCULATE threshold condition
positive_cases.input: Large Sales = CALCULATE( SUM(Sales[Amount]), FILTER( Sales, Sales[Amount] > 1000 ) )
positive_cases.expected_issue: Using FILTER incorrectly for scalar comparison

positive_cases.expected_issue: Division without zero handling

positive_cases.expected_issue: IF instead of DIVIDE

positive_cases.expected_issue: VALUES used where scalar expected

positive_cases.expected_issue: DISTINCT used where scalar expected

positive_cases.expected_issue: Using MAX instead of SELECTEDVALUE

positive_cases.expected_issue: Incorrect use of CALCULATE without filters

positive_cases.expected_issue: Nested CALCULATE calls

positive_cases.expected_issue: Using ALL removes required filters

positive_cases.expected_issue: Incorrect use of ALLSELECTED for totals

positive_cases.name: COUNT used on table
positive_cases.expected_issue: COUNT used on table instead of COUNTROWS

positive_cases.name: COUNTROWS VALUES distinct order count
positive_cases.expected_issue: Using COUNTROWS(VALUES()) instead of DISTINCTCOUNT

positive_cases.name: SUM used on identifier column
positive_cases.expected_issue: SUM used on non-numeric column

positive_cases.name: EARLIER rank pattern in measure
positive_cases.input: Sales Rank = CALCULATE(COUNTROWS(Sales), FILTER(Sales, Sales[Amount] > EARLIER(Sales[Amount])))
positive_cases.expected_issue: Incorrect use of EARLIER in measure

positive_cases.name: BLANK equality comparison
positive_cases.expected_issue: Incorrect logical comparison with BLANK

positive_cases.name: OR filter should use IN
positive_cases.input: US EU Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), Sales[Region] = "US" || Sales[Region] = "EU")
positive_cases.expected_issue: Incorrect OR condition using multiple filters
positive_cases.expected_fix: US EU Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), Sales[Region] IN {"US", "EU"})

positive_cases.name: Conflicting filters on same column
positive_cases.input: North South Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), Sales[Region] = "North", Sales[Region] = "South")
positive_cases.expected_issue: Multiple filters overriding unintentionally
positive_cases.expected_fix: North South Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), Sales[Region] IN {"North", "South"})

positive_cases.name: Duplicate CALCULATE filter
positive_cases.expected_issue: Redundant filters in CALCULATE

positive_cases.expected_issue: LASTDATE used as scalar date value

positive_cases.name: Manual YTD filter
positive_cases.expected_issue: Incorrect time intelligence using manual filter

positive_cases.name: FILTER TRUE in CALCULATE
positive_cases.expected_issue: Using FILTER on entire table unnecessarily

positive_cases.name: CALCULATETABLE materialized for scalar sum
positive_cases.expected_issue: CALCULATETABLE used where CALCULATE scalar is sufficient

positive_cases.name: USERELATIONSHIP inside SUMX
positive_cases.expected_issue: USERELATIONSHIP inside iterator

positive_cases.name: Repeated measure in SWITCH branches
positive_cases.expected_issue: Measure evaluated repeatedly inside SWITCH branches
positive_cases.expected_fix: Revenue Segment = VAR RevenueValue = [Revenue] RETURN SWITCH(TRUE(), RevenueValue > 1000000, "High", RevenueValue > 500000, "Medium", RevenueValue > 0, "Low", "None")

positive_cases.name: SELECTCOLUMNS before count
positive_cases.expected_issue: SELECTCOLUMNS projection used before simple count

positive_cases.name: GROUPBY CURRENTGROUP simple aggregation
positive_cases.input: Grouped Sales = SUMX(GROUPBY(Sales, Sales[Region], "Amt", SUMX(CURRENTGROUP(), Sales[Amount])), [Amt])
positive_cases.expected_issue: GROUPBY with CURRENTGROUP for simple aggregation

positive_cases.name: ALLEXCEPT over wide fact table
positive_cases.expected_issue: Overuse of ALLEXCEPT on wide fact table

negative_cases.name: Weighted average with SUMX
negative_cases.input: Weighted Price = DIVIDE(SUMX(Sales, Sales[Quantity] * Sales[UnitPrice]), SUM(Sales[Quantity]))

negative_cases.name: FILTER valid dimension threshold
negative_cases.input: High Value Customer Sales = CALCULATE([Revenue], FILTER(Customer, [Revenue] > 10000))

negative_cases.name: DATESINPERIOD on date table
negative_cases.input: Rolling 30 Sales = CALCULATE([Revenue], DATESINPERIOD('Date'[Date], MAX('Date'[Date]), -30, DAY))

negative_cases.name: IN syntax already correct
negative_cases.input: US EU Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), Sales[Region] IN {"US", "EU"})

negative_cases.name: SWITCH TRUE with cached measure
negative_cases.input: Revenue Segment = VAR RevenueValue = [Revenue] RETURN SWITCH(TRUE(), RevenueValue > 1000000, "High", RevenueValue > 500000, "Medium", RevenueValue > 0, "Low", "None")

negative_cases.name: CROSSFILTER intentional one-way override
negative_cases.input: One Way Sales = CALCULATE([Revenue], CROSSFILTER(Customer[CustomerID], Sales[CustomerID], ONEWAY))

negative_cases.name: LOOKUPVALUE with unique alternate result
negative_cases.input: Standard Cost = LOOKUPVALUE(Product[StandardCost], Product[ProductID], Sales[ProductID], BLANK())

edge_cases.name: SUMX FILTER compound AND predicate
edge_cases.input: Qualified Sales = SUMX(FILTER(Sales, Sales[Amount] > 100 && Sales[Region] = "US"), Sales[Amount])
edge_cases.expected_issue: SUMX over filtered fact table with direct additive column

edge_cases.name: SUMX FILTER OR predicate preservation
edge_cases.input: Priority Sales = SUMX(FILTER(Sales, Sales[Status] = "Open" || Sales[Status] = "Pending"), Sales[Amount])
edge_cases.expected_issue: SUMX over filtered fact table with direct additive column

edge_cases.name: SUMX FILTER with different filter and sum columns
edge_cases.input: Positive Qty Sales = SUMX(FILTER(Sales, Sales[Quantity] > 0), Sales[Amount])
edge_cases.expected_issue: SUMX over filtered fact table with direct additive column

edge_cases.name: FILTER CALCULATE with two conditions
edge_cases.input: US Large Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), FILTER(Sales, Sales[Amount] > 500 && Sales[Region] = "US"))
edge_cases.expected_issue: Using FILTER incorrectly for scalar comparison
edge_cases.expected_fix: US Large Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), Sales[Amount] > 500 && Sales[Region] = "US")

edge_cases.name: FILTER CALCULATE preserves OR logic
edge_cases.input: Active Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), FILTER(Sales, Sales[Status] = "Open" || Sales[Status] = "Closed"))
edge_cases.expected_issue: Using FILTER incorrectly for scalar comparison

edge_cases.name: Division expression with parentheses
edge_cases.input: Profit Rate = (SUM(Sales[Revenue]) - SUM(Sales[Cost])) / SUM(Sales[Revenue])
edge_cases.expected_issue: Division without zero handling

edge_cases.name: Division expression with CALCULATE denominator
edge_cases.input: Category Share = [Revenue] / CALCULATE([Revenue], REMOVEFILTERS(Product[Category]))
edge_cases.expected_issue: Division without zero handling

edge_cases.name: VALUES scalar inside IF
edge_cases.input: Product Label = IF(HASONEVALUE(Product[ProductName]), VALUES(Product[ProductName]), "Multiple")
edge_cases.expected_issue: VALUES used where scalar expected

edge_cases.name: Nested CALCULATE with valid outer filter
edge_cases.input: US Revenue = CALCULATE(CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount])), Sales[Region] = "US")
edge_cases.expected_issue: Nested CALCULATE calls

edge_cases.name: ALL removes only fact table in ratio
edge_cases.input: Bad Share = DIVIDE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), ALL(Sales)))
edge_cases.expected_issue: Using ALL removes required filters

edge_cases.name: ALLSELECTED with full fact table
edge_cases.input: Selected Total = CALCULATE([Revenue], ALLSELECTED(Sales))
edge_cases.expected_issue: Incorrect use of ALLSELECTED for totals

edge_cases.name: Duplicate filters with messy whitespace
edge_cases.input: Retail Sales = CALCULATE( SUM(Sales[Amount]) , Sales[Channel] = "Retail" , Sales[Channel] = "Retail" )
edge_cases.expected_issue: Redundant filters in CALCULATE

edge_cases.name: Conflicting date type filters
edge_cases.input: Order Ship Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), Sales[DateType] = "Order", Sales[DateType] = "Ship")
edge_cases.expected_issue: Multiple filters overriding unintentionally
edge_cases.expected_fix: Order Ship Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), Sales[DateType] IN {"Order", "Ship"})

edge_cases.name: Manual YTD with TODAY must preserve date table
edge_cases.input: Current YTD Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), FILTER(ALL('Date'), 'Date'[Date] <= TODAY()))
edge_cases.expected_issue: Incorrect time intelligence using manual filter

edge_cases.name: EARLIER in calculated rank with customer granularity
edge_cases.input: Customer Rank = CALCULATE(COUNTROWS(Customer), FILTER(Customer, Customer[Revenue] > EARLIER(Customer[Revenue])))
edge_cases.expected_issue: Incorrect use of EARLIER in measure

edge_cases.name: LOOKUPVALUE multiple match risk
edge_cases.input: Customer Segment = LOOKUPVALUE(Segment[SegmentName], Segment[CustomerID], Sales[CustomerID])
edge_cases.expected_issue: Incorrect use of LOOKUPVALUE with multiple matches

edge_cases.name: INTERSECT virtual relationship
edge_cases.input: Matched Revenue = CALCULATE([Revenue], INTERSECT(VALUES(Customer[CustomerID]), VALUES(Sales[CustomerID])))
edge_cases.expected_issue: Bi-directional virtual relationship using INTERSECT

edge_cases.name: CONTAINS virtual relationship inside FILTER
edge_cases.input: Customer Revenue = CALCULATE([Revenue], FILTER(Sales, CONTAINS(VALUES(Customer[CustomerID]), Customer[CustomerID], Sales[CustomerID])))
edge_cases.expected_issue: Virtual relationship using CONTAINS

edge_cases.name: SUMMARIZE before TOPN ranking
edge_cases.input: Top Product Sales = SUMX(TOPN(10, SUMMARIZE(Sales, Product[ProductName], "Sales", [Revenue]), [Sales], DESC), [Sales])
edge_cases.expected_issue: SUMMARIZE used before TOPN ranking
edge_cases.expected_fix: Top Product Sales = SUMX(TOPN(10, ALL(Product[ProductName]), [Revenue], DESC), [Revenue])

edge_cases.name: UNION filtered fact tables
edge_cases.input: Open Closed Sales = SUMX(UNION(FILTER(Sales, Sales[Status] = "Open"), FILTER(Sales, Sales[Status] = "Closed")), Sales[Amount])
edge_cases.expected_issue: UNION of filtered fact tables inside measure
edge_cases.expected_fix: Open Closed Sales = CALCULATE(SUM(Sales[Amount]), Sales[Status] IN {"Open", "Closed"})
